blocks:
Ayahs

سَأَلَ سَآئِلٌۢ بِعَذَابٍ وَاقِعٍ (۱)
An asker asked for a punishment sure to befall (1)
لِّلْكَـٰفِرِينَ لَيْسَ لَهُۥ دَافِعٌ (۲)
—which none can avert from the faithless — (2)
مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ذِى ٱلْمَعَارِجِ (۳)
from Allah, Lord of the lofty stations. (3)
تَعْرُجُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ وَٱلرُّوحُ إِلَيْهِ فِى يَوْمٍ كَانَ مِقْدَارُهُۥ خَمْسِينَ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ (٤)
The angels and the Spirit ascend to Him in a day whose span is fifty thousand years. (4)

Key Points

Ma'arij (معارج) is the plural of Ma'raj (مَعرج), meaning the place of ascent (urūj) and rising (su'ūd). What is meant by it are the heavens, which are the place of descent and ascent for the angels. The late Allamah Amini (قدس سره) mentioned in his noble book Al-Ghadir (1) the names of thirty great scholars of exegesis (mufassirīn) and tradition (muhaddithīn), from the third century Hijri until the fourteenth century, who stated that: The occasion of revelation (sha'n nuzūl) of the Ayah "سأل سائل" relates to a person who said to the Prophet: Was the appointment of Amir al-Mu'minin (عليه السلام) to the Caliphate on the Day of Ghadir from yourself or from Allah?! You commanded us regarding Hajj, fasting, and Zakat, and we accepted, but you were not satisfied until you appointed your own cousin as ruler over us! Then he said: "اللّهم اِن كان هذا هو الحقّ من عندك فامطر علینا حجارة من السماء" (2) (O God, if this matter is the truth from You, then rain down a stone upon us from the sky [so that we may be destroyed and not witness such a day]). A stone descended, and he was destroyed, and this Ayah was revealed: "سأل سائل بعذاب واقع".

1) Al-Ghadir, Vol. 1, pp. 239 to 246. 2) Al-Anfal, 32.

Messages

1- Recalling certain minor and individual incidents is necessary for the admonition of others and must be preserved. (The questioner was one person whom Allah's wrath destroyed, but this incident is presented in the text of the Quran so that all human beings throughout history may read it.) "سأل سائل بعذاب واقع"

2- An inappropriate, malicious, and stubborn request can lead to a catastrophe. "سأل سائل بعذاب واقع"

3- As long as the Divine punishment has not descended, the path of repentance and averting punishment through charity (sadaqah) is open, but a punishment that is in the process of descending has no means of escape. "بعذاب واقع... لیس له دافع"

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Ma'arij

This Surah has forty-four Ayahs and is among the Makkan Surahs, although its initial Ayahs were revealed in Medina. The name of the Surah is taken from the third Ayah and means the place of ascent for the angels in the heavens. Like other Makkan Surahs, the central theme of the Ayahs in this Surah revolves around the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and the warning of the polytheists and opponents. The states of the disbelievers on the Day of Judgment and the attributes and characteristics of the people of Paradise and the people of Hell form another part of the Surah's Ayahs. However, the initial Ayahs of the Surah concern the descent of punishment in this world, which afflicted one of the deniers of the Wilayah (Guardianship) of Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام), leading to his destruction. The details of the incident are mentioned under the first Ayah.

Ayahs

فَٱصْبِرْ صَبْرًا جَمِيلًا (٥)
So be patient, with a patience that is graceful. (5)
إِنَّهُمْ يَرَوْنَهُۥ بَعِيدًا (٦)
Indeed they see it to be far off, (6)
وَنَرَىٰهُ قَرِيبًا (۷)
and We see it to be near. (7)
يَوْمَ تَكُونُ ٱلسَّمَآءُ كَٱلْمُهْلِ (۸)
The day when the sky will be like molten copper, (8)
وَتَكُونُ ٱلْجِبَالُ كَٱلْعِهْنِ (۹)
and the mountains like [tufts of] dyed wool, (9)

Key Points

The stings and bitterness directed by some Muslims and opponents towards the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, regarding the event of installing Imam Ali عليه السلام to the Imamate at Ghadir Khumm, were so intense that Allah commanded His Prophet to be patient. «سأل سائل بعذاب... فاصبر صبراً جمیلاً» The disbelievers consider the principle of the Resurrection (Ma'ad) to be a distant matter, deeming it far from imagination and reason, whereas, in the sight of Allah, the Day of Judgment is a certain and inevitable event; thus, it is a near matter. «انهم یرونه بعیدا و نراهُ قریبا» Allah has invited His Prophet to patience about twenty times in the Quran, specifically Sabran Jamil (beautiful patience), which is free from anxiety, distress, and inappropriate speech. Of course, the address regarding patience is directed to the Prophet, but the Ummah (community) must also possess patience. For the establishment of the Day of Judgment, the existing order of the earth and the heavens will be completely transformed. There are expressions in the Quran that describe this disruption, as He says: «یوم نطوى السماء كطىّ السجلّ للكتب»(3) We shall roll up the heaven like the rolling up of a scroll for writings.(4)

3) Al-Anbiya, 104. 4) For further detail, refer to the author's book on the Resurrection (Ma'ad).

Messages

1- Even the Prophet is in need of counsel regarding patience and steadfastness. «فاصبر صبراً جمیلاً»

2- Whoever sees the Day of Judgment as near will show patience and steadfastness in the face of difficulties. «فاصبر... نراه قریباً»

3- The existing order in the heavens has an end and will one day be destroyed. «یوم تكون السّماء كالمهل»

Ayahs

وَلَا يَسْـَٔلُ حَمِيمٌ حَمِيمًا (۱٠)
and no friend will inquire about [the welfare of his] friend, (10)
يُبَصَّرُونَهُمْ ۚ يَوَدُّ ٱلْمُجْرِمُ لَوْ يَفْتَدِى مِنْ عَذَابِ يَوْمِئِذٍۭ بِبَنِيهِ (۱۱)
[though] they will be placed within each other’s sight. The guilty one will wish he could ransom himself from the punishment of that day at the price of his children, (11)
وَصَـٰحِبَتِهِۦ وَأَخِيهِ (۱۲)
his spouse and his brother, (12)
وَفَصِيلَتِهِ ٱلَّتِى تُـْٔوِيهِ (۱۳)
his kin, which had sheltered him (13)
وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ يُنجِيهِ (۱٤)
and all those who are upon the earth, if that might deliver him. (14)
كَلَّآ ۖ إِنَّهَا لَظَىٰ (۱٥)
Never! Indeed, it is a blazing fire, (15)
نَزَّاعَةً لِّلشَّوَىٰ (۱٦)
which strips away the scalp. (16)
تَدْعُوا۟ مَنْ أَدْبَرَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ (۱۷)
It invites him, who has turned back [from the truth] and forsaken [it], (17)
وَجَمَعَ فَأَوْعَىٰٓ (۱۸)
amassing [wealth] and hoarding [it]. (18)

Key Points

Yaftadā (یَفتدى) means giving ransom (fidyah) or compensation for release and salvation. Faṣīlah (فَصیلة) means the family from which a person has separated. Tu'wīh (تؤیه) is from ma'wā, meaning to give refuge. Laẓā (لَظى) is the pure flame of the fire. Aw‘ā (اَوعى) is from wi‘ā’, meaning a container, and the intent is storing something in the container. Shawā (شَوى) refers to the skin surrounding the body.

The criminal makes three wishes on the Day of Judgment: a) That he be leveled with the earth. «لو تُسوّى بهم الارض»(5) b) That he be far removed from his deeds. «امداً بعیداً»(6) c) That he be released by giving ransom. «یوم یودّ المجرم لو یفتدى...»

The criminal does not choose an individual or a group for ransom and salvation; rather, he says: Take everyone and free me: his son, his spouse, his brother, his family, and all people on earth. (The words are conjoined with the letter wāw [and], not with the letter aw [or].)

On that Day, filial affection, spousal devotion, brotherly love, familial support, and acquaintance with people are all sacrificed—but to what avail?!

Based on these verses, the factors leading to Hellfire are four things: 1. The individual turns his back on the truth outwardly: adbara. 2. He harbors hatred in his heart and turns away: tawallā. 3. He hoards wealth: jama‘a. 4. And he does not give to others. fa-aw‘ā.

5) Nisa, 42. 6) Āl ‘Imrān, 30.

Messages

1- Do not become attached to worldly friendships and warm relationships, for they will be useless in the Hereafter. «لا یسئل حمیم حمیماً»

2- On the Day of Judgment, spiritual and physical torture occur simultaneously. Showing one's close relatives and intimate friends to the person, and vice versa, is the highest spiritual torture. «یبصّرونهم»

3- On that Day, the criminal desires his salvation from the torment at the cost of the destruction of everyone. «یودّ المجرم لو یفتدى... ببنیه و صاحبته و اخیه... و من فى الارض جمیعاً ثمّ ینجیه»

4- We must be careful not to condemn ourselves to Hell in this world for the sake of the welfare of our spouses and children or to gain the satisfaction of friends and relatives. For on that Day, none of them will come to our aid or be of any use to us. «لو یفتدى... ببنیه و صاحبته و اخیه»

5- The instinct of self-preservation (love of self) is the highest of instincts. «لو یفتدى... من فى الارض جمیعاً ثمّ ینجیه»

6- The Fire of Hell has consciousness and recognizes the criminal. «تدعوا من ادبر و تولّى »

Ayahs

۞ إِنَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ خُلِقَ هَلُوعًا (۱۹)
Indeed man has been created covetous: (19)
إِذَا مَسَّهُ ٱلشَّرُّ جَزُوعًا (۲٠)
anxious when an ill befalls him (20)
وَإِذَا مَسَّهُ ٱلْخَيْرُ مَنُوعًا (۲۱)
and grudging [charity] when good comes his way (21)
إِلَّا ٱلْمُصَلِّينَ (۲۲)
—[all are such] except the prayerful, (22)
ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ عَلَىٰ صَلَاتِهِمْ دَآئِمُونَ (۲۳)
those who persevere in their prayers (23)

Key Points

Halū‘ (هَلوع) means an impatient and greedy person. Question: In some verses, Allah describes the creation of man as the best: «خلقنا الانسان فى احسن تقویم» (7) and describes Himself as the best Creator: «احسن الخالقین» (8). However, in these verses, it is explicitly stated that man was created greedy and stingy. How can these two descriptions be reconciled? Answer: Contradictory elements have been placed in the creation of man, and this is the secret of perfection (or development). The nafs (soul/self) drives man toward evil: «انّ النّفس لأمّارة بالسّوء» (9), but the intellect restrains him from it: «نَهى النّفس عن الهَوى » (10). Man's growth lies in choosing between these oppositions, which leads to his refinement and purification (tazkiyah). Based on narrations, the best deed is that which is continuous (11). «الّذین هم على صلاتهم دائمون» Many narrations have been reported regarding greed (12), some of which we mention:

«الحرص لا یزید فى الرزق» Greed does not increase sustenance (rizq). «مَن كثر حرصه... ذلّ قدره» Whoever has more greed... his status becomes humiliated and abased. «رُبَّ حریص قتله حرصه» How often has a greedy person been killed by his own greed. «لیس لحریص غناء» A greedy person is never self-sufficient. «الحرص یُرزى بالمروة» Greed damages chivalry (murūwwah) and generosity. «الحرص مطیّة التّعب» Greed is the mount of fatigue (or the vehicle of suffering). «لا حَیاء لحریص» A greedy person has no modesty (hayā’). «الحریص الاسیر» The greedy person is a captive. «الحرص علامة الفقر» Greed is a sign of poverty. «الحرص یفسد الایقان» Greed corrupts certainty (īqān) in Divine decrees. «الحرص موقع فى كثیر العیوب» Greed causes man to fall into many faults. «لا یلقى الحریص مُستریحاً» The greedy person never finds rest or comfort.

7) At-Tin, 4. 8) Al-Mu’minūn, 14. 9) Yūsuf, 53. 10) An-Nāzi‘āt, 40. 11) Tafsir Nūr ath-Thaqalayn. 12) Ghurar al-Hikam.

Messages

1- The signs of being greedy are two things: impatience (jaza‘) during hardship and stinginess (bukhl) during prosperity. «هلوعاً اذا مسّه الشر جزوعاً و اذا مسّه الخیر منوعاً»

2- The root of many abnormalities (or moral deviations) is greed and stinginess. «جزوعاً... منوعاً»

3- Man has very little capacity; upon contact with hardship, he cries out, and he acts stingily. «مسّه... مسّه»

4- The path to salvation from undesirable morals is persistence in prayer (Salat). «خلق هلوعاً... الاّ المصلّین»

5- Man's material nature tends toward greed and stinginess, and prayer and the remembrance of Allah control it. «انّ الانسان خلق هلوعاً... الاّ المصلّین»

6- Man without prayer is anxious. «جزوعاً... منوعاً... الاّ المصلّین»

7- The effects of prayer depend on persistence in it. «الّذین هم على صلاتهم دائمون»

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ فِىٓ أَمْوَٰلِهِمْ حَقٌّ مَّعْلُومٌ (۲٤)
and there is a known share in whose wealth (24)
لِّلسَّآئِلِ وَٱلْمَحْرُومِ (۲٥)
for the beggar and the deprived, (25)
وَٱلَّذِينَ يُصَدِّقُونَ بِيَوْمِ ٱلدِّينِ (۲٦)
and who affirm the Day of Retribution, (26)
وَٱلَّذِينَ هُم مِّنْ عَذَابِ رَبِّهِم مُّشْفِقُونَ (۲۷)
and those who are apprehensive of the punishment of their Lord (27)
إِنَّ عَذَابَ رَبِّهِمْ غَيْرُ مَأْمُونٍ (۲۸)
(there is indeed no security from the punishment of their Lord) (28)
وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ لِفُرُوجِهِمْ حَـٰفِظُونَ (۲۹)
and those who guard their private parts (29)
إِلَّا عَلَىٰٓ أَزْوَٰجِهِمْ أَوْ مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ غَيْرُ مَلُومِينَ (۳٠)
(apart from their spouses and their slave women, for then they are not blameworthy; (30)
فَمَنِ ٱبْتَغَىٰ وَرَآءَ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْعَادُونَ (۳۱)
but whoever seeks beyond that—it is they who are the transgressors) (31)

Key Points

According to commentators, this Ayah was revealed before the obligation of Khums (one-fifth) and Zakat (alms). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked about the "known right" (حقّ معلوم). He said: This right is other than the obligatory Zakat, and its meaning is that Allah has given wealth to a person, and he sets aside a portion of it, using it to maintain kinship ties (Silat al-Rahm) and relieve hardship from his relatives. (13) Imam Baqir عليه السلام was also asked about this Ayah, and He said: The "known right" (حق معلوم) is other than Zakat. (14) We read in Nahj al-Balaghah: «انّ اللّه سبحانه فرض فى اموال الاغنیاء اقوات الفقراء فما جاع فقیر الاّ بما متّع به غنىّ و اللّه تعالى سائلهم عن ذلك» (15) Indeed, Allah has placed the sustenance of the poor in the wealth of the rich, and no poor person goes hungry except because a rich person has enjoyed his right, and Allah will hold them accountable for that. The phrase «للسائل و المحروم» (for the beggar and the deprived) indicates that some needy people in society do not ask and do not show their deprivation. Therefore, it is incumbent upon the wealthy to seek them out, identify them, and pay their rights.

One must resist inappropriate customs and sensitivities and not portray what Allah has made lawful as unlawful. In society, there are certain customs and traditions that prevent the timely marriage of young men and women or prevent the bride and groom from living together, and if marriage occurs early in youth, people blame and criticize them. Whereas, according to the Quran, these individuals should not be blamed, and those who seek means other than marriage should be criticized.

Masturbation (Istimna') is a great sin. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked about masturbation. He said: «اثم عظیم نهى اللّه عنه فى كتابه» (A great sin which Allah has forbidden in His Book). They asked: In which Ayah? He recited the Ayah: «فمن ابتغى وراء ذلك فاولئك هم العادون». (16)

Imam Ali عليه السلام said to his son: Fear Allah in such a way that even if you possess all good deeds, you might still assume He will not accept them; and hope in Him in such a way that even if you possess all the evil deeds of the people of the earth, you might still assume He will forgive you. (17)

Belief in the Day of Judgment and fear of punishment are the foundation for attention to prayer (Salat) and spending (Infaq).

13) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 14) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 92, p. 95. 15) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 328. 16) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 101, p. 101. 17) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

  1. The halu' (impatient/anxious) possesses anxiety and stinginess, but the musalli (one who prays) possesses prayer and spending (infaq). «هلوعاً... جزوعاً.. منوعا - الاّ المصلّین... حقّ معلوم للسائل و المحروم»
  2. Prayer and caring for the deprived have an inseparable link. «الاّ المصلّین... حقّ معلوم للسائل و المحروم»
  3. Spending (Infaq) is a moderator of the spirit of greed and stinginess. «خلق هلوعا... جمع فاوعى... فى اموالهم حقّ معلوم»
  4. The true believer and worshipper does not consider himself the owner of all his possessions but regards a share of it as the right of others. «فى اموالهم حقّ معلوم»
  5. The needy have a right and are partners in the wealth of the affluent. «حقّ معلوم للسائل والمحروم»
  6. In poverty alleviation, do not look only at the outward appearance of individuals; a group of the deprived keep their faces red with slaps (i.e., hide their poverty) and do not ask. «و المحروم»
  7. The happiness of the individual is not separate from the happiness of society. «المصلّین... للسائل و المحروم»
  8. Need is the condition for receiving, whether the person is a believer or not. «للسائل و المحروم»
  9. The comprehensive religion is one that pays attention both to social issues, «حقّ معلوم للسائل و المحروم», and to doctrinal issues. «الّذین یصدّقون بیوم الدّین»
  10. Islam is a natural religion (Fitri). Instincts must be satisfied through the correct path, and immorality and evil must be prevented. «الّذین هم لفروجهم حافظون... الاّ على ازواجهم»
  11. Islam is opposed to the rejection of the sexual instinct. «الاّ على ازواجهم»
  12. Danger is always lurking: the danger of deviation, a bad end, dying without repentance, the danger of deeds not being accepted, the danger of deeds being nullified, and so on. «و الّذین هم من عذاب ربّهم مشفقون»
  13. In lawful marriage, there should be no blame upon anyone, regardless of time, place, or age. «فانهم غیر ملومین»
  14. Satisfying the sexual instinct through unlawful means is a form of transgression. «فمن ابتغى وراء ذلك فاولئك هم العادون»

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ لِأَمَـٰنَـٰتِهِمْ وَعَهْدِهِمْ رَٰعُونَ (۳۲)
and those who keep their trusts and covenants, (32)
وَٱلَّذِينَ هُم بِشَهَـٰدَٰتِهِمْ قَآئِمُونَ (۳۳)
and those who are observant of their testimonies, (33)
وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ عَلَىٰ صَلَاتِهِمْ يُحَافِظُونَ (۳٤)
and those who are watchful of their prayers. (34)
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى جَنَّـٰتٍ مُّكْرَمُونَ (۳٥)
They will be in gardens, held in honour. (35)

Key Points

In verses 22 to 35 of Surah Al-Ma'arij, the portrait of the Islamic Ummah and the ideal society is drawn: They maintain a permanent and complete relationship with Allah through prayer (Salat). They pay attention to the problems of the deprived and consider a portion of their wealth to be the right of those people. Due to their belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and fear of the reckoning, piety (Taqwa) governs their thoughts, speech, and behavior. They are committed to their trusts and covenants. They stand up for the rights of the people, and if they are called upon to bear witness or testify, they do not refuse. In satisfying the sexual instinct, they seek the lawful way, forming a family and producing pure offspring. Trust (Amanah) also includes spiritual trusts, such as responsibilities, people's secrets, and their honor. Every covenant and agreement is binding: with people, with Allah, with religious leaders, with one's spouse, and so on. Perhaps the meaning of the phrase «على صلاتهم دائمون» (18) is not abandoning [prayer], and the meaning of «على صلاتهم یحافظون» is taking prayer seriously. Such as awaiting the prayer, performing ablution (Wudu) before the time arrives, praying in congregation and in the mosque, and observing the conditions for the validity, acceptance, and perfection of the prayer. (19) In a narration from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, breach of trust, failure to fulfill a covenant, and lying are counted among the signs of hypocrisy (Nifaq). (20)

18) Ma'arij, 23. 19) Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr Razi. 20) Safinat al-Bihar, Vol. 2, p. 131.

Messages

1- The sign of faith is not only prayer and charity; observing social covenants and agreements is also a condition of faith. «و الّذین هم لاماناتهم و عهدهم راعون»

2- The silence of the knowledgeable is a crime, and testifying to the truth is a sign of faith. «بشهاداتهم قائمون»

3- The beginning and end of all perfections is attention to prayer. In the beginning, He said: «الّذین هم على صلاتهم دائمون», and at the end, He also says: «و الّذین هم على صلاتهم یحافظون»

4- The people of Paradise are provided for both materially, «فى جنّاتٍ», and spiritually, «مكرمون».

Ayahs

فَمَالِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ قِبَلَكَ مُهْطِعِينَ (۳٦)
What is the matter with the faithless that they scramble toward you, (36)
عَنِ ٱلْيَمِينِ وَعَنِ ٱلشِّمَالِ عِزِينَ (۳۷)
from left and right, in groups? (37)
أَيَطْمَعُ كُلُّ ٱمْرِئٍ مِّنْهُمْ أَن يُدْخَلَ جَنَّةَ نَعِيمٍ (۳۸)
Does each man among them hope to enter the garden of bliss? (38)
كَلَّآ ۖ إِنَّا خَلَقْنَـٰهُم مِّمَّا يَعْلَمُونَ (۳۹)
Never! Indeed, We created them from what they know. (39)

Key Points

Muhtai'een (مُهطِعین) means craning the neck, staring intently, or walking quickly in search of something. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: The noble Messenger of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله continuously showed forbearance towards the hypocrites and seated them to His right and His left, until Allah permitted Him to distance them from Himself, and He cited this Ayah: «فمال الّذین كفروا قبلك مهطعین».(21) This is because kufr (disbelief) means non-submission, not non-recognition. Just as Satan recognized God and accepted the Resurrection, for he said: «أنظرنى الى یوم یبعثون»(22) (Grant me respite till the Day they are resurrected), yet despite this, he did not submit to Allah. In any case, knowledge is different from faith. The hypocrites had recognition of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله but were not submissive to Him, and thus the title Kafir (disbeliever) is applied to them. Qibal (قِبَل) means facing, and ‘Izeen (عِزین) is the plural of ‘Izzah (عِزّة), meaning scattered groups. On the morning of Ashura, Umar ibn Sa'd, intending to kill Imam Husayn عليه السلام, told his army: «یا خیل اللّه اركبى و أبشرى بالجنّة»(23) (O cavalry of Allah, mount up and receive the good news of Paradise). The disbelievers mockingly said: If the Prophet's words about Paradise are true, we too are people of Paradise, and we will even go there sooner. «أیطمع كلّ امرءٍ منهم ان یُدخل جنّةَ نعیم»

(21) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. (22) Al-A'raf, 14. (23) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The Quran possessed an attraction such that even opponents gathered around the Prophet to hear it, craning their necks. «فمال الّذین كفروا قبلك مهطعین»

2- The Prophet had such broad-mindedness (tolerance) that even opponents gathered around Him. «عن الیمین و عن الشمال عزین»

3- Do not be overly pleased by every gathering or warm welcome from the people. «مهطعین عن الیمین و عن الشمال عزین»

4- It is possible that the enemy may be present around the believers to gather news and information about their decisions. «فمال الّذین كفروا... عن الیمین و عن الشمال عزین»

5- The disbelievers (Kuffar) turned away from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, but it was the hypocrites who drew themselves closer and closer to the Prophet. «عن الیمین و عن الشمال عزین»

6- The hypocrite is proud of his actions and covets Paradise. «یطمع كلّ امرء منهم ان یُدخل جنّة نعیم»

7- The hypocrites expect to enter Paradise without faith and good deeds. «یُدخَل جنّة نعیم»

8- Respond decisively to the enemies and their misplaced hopes. «یطمع... كلاّ»

9- Those who know that We created them from a despised fluid, why do they possess such arrogance that, despite their disbelief and hypocrisy, they expect Paradise? «كلاّ انّا خلقناهم ممّا یعلمون»

10- Those who know what We created them from must also know that We have the power to create them again. «انّا خلقناهم مما یعلمون»

Ayahs

فَلَآ أُقْسِمُ بِرَبِّ ٱلْمَشَـٰرِقِ وَٱلْمَغَـٰرِبِ إِنَّا لَقَـٰدِرُونَ (٤٠)
So I swear by the Lord of the easts and the wests that We are able (40)
عَلَىٰٓ أَن نُّبَدِّلَ خَيْرًا مِّنْهُمْ وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمَسْبُوقِينَ (٤۱)
to replace them with [others] better than them and We are not to be outmaneuvered. (41)
فَذَرْهُمْ يَخُوضُوا۟ وَيَلْعَبُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يُلَـٰقُوا۟ يَوْمَهُمُ ٱلَّذِى يُوعَدُونَ (٤۲)
So leave them to gossip and play till they encounter the day they are promised: (42)
يَوْمَ يَخْرُجُونَ مِنَ ٱلْأَجْدَاثِ سِرَاعًا كَأَنَّهُمْ إِلَىٰ نُصُبٍ يُوفِضُونَ (٤۳)
the day when they emerge from graves, hastening, as if racing toward a target, (43)
خَـٰشِعَةً أَبْصَـٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يُوعَدُونَ (٤٤)
with a humbled look [in their eyes], overcast by abasement. That is the day they had been promised. (44)

Key Points

The intent of "المشارق و المغارب" (the Easts and the Wests) is the sun's easts and wests, because the sun has a separate rising and setting point on every day of the year. It rises from one point and sets at another, and it never rises from yesterday's east or sets in yesterday's west. (24) The intent of "خیراً منهم" (better than them) is superiority in faith. Meaning, We are capable of removing the hypocrites and replacing them with people of faith who follow the truth and do not deny it. Regarding the East and the West, three types of expressions are used in the Quran: singular: "المشرق و المغرب" (25), dual: "المشرقَین و المغربَین" (26), and plural: "المشارق و المغارب". In a general view, there is one direction for the East and one direction for the West. In another view, it refers to the change in orbit during summer and winter, where the summer orbit is in the north and the winter orbit is in the south; thus, the sun has two easts and two wests. In a more precise view, every day has a unique point of rising and setting, which effectively results in 365 easts and 365 wests. A tradition narrated from Imam Ali عليه السلام also confirms this meaning. (27) Similar to Ayah 41, Allah has also threatened the disbelievers, polytheists, and hypocrites in other verses that He is capable of removing them and bringing forth better people than them: "ان یشاء یذهبكم و یأت بخلق جدید" (28), "ان تتولوا یستبدل قوماً غیركم ثم لا یكونوا امثالكم" (29) Ayah 42 of this Surah is similar to Ayah 83 of Surah Az-Zukhruf. "فذرهم یخوضوا..." The emergence from the graves is accompanied by haste and confusion. "یخرجون من الاجداث سراعا" Just as He says elsewhere: "یخرجون من الاجداث كانّهم جراد منتشر" (30) or "من الاجداث الى ربّهم ینسلون" (31) Nusub (نُصُب) refers to a marker erected on roads so that the way is not lost. Of course, some commentators, considering the verse "و ما ذبح على النُّصب" (32), which concerns sacrificing at the foot of idols, have interpreted Nusub in this Ayah also to mean idols, but this meaning is far removed from the word of Allah. (33) In Surah Al-Qalam, Ayah 44, we read: "فذَرنى و مَن یكذّب بهذا الحدیث سنستدرجهم من حیث لا یعلمون و املى لهم انّ كیدى مَتین" (So leave Me and him who denies this discourse. We will gradually seize them from where they do not know, and I will grant them respite, for My plan is firm.) Taking a stand against the Prophet leads to humiliation in this world: "یحادّون اللّه و رسوله اولئك فى الاذلّین" (34) and in the Hereafter. "ترهقهم ذلّة" Those who intended to harm the Prophet with their eyes in this world, "لیزلقونك بابصارهم" (35), will have downcast and ashamed eyes in the Hereafter. "خاشعة ابصارهم"

24) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 25) Al-Baqarah, 115. 26) Ar-Rahman, 17. 27) Tafsir Nur Ath-Thaqalayn. 28) Fatir, 16. 29) Muhammad, 38. 30) Al-Qamar, 7. 31) Ya Sin, 51. 32) Al-Ma'idah, 3. 33) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 34) Al-Mujadilah, 20. 35) Al-Qalam, 51.

Messages

1- Allah's oath by the Easts and the Wests should compel man to reflect upon them. "فلا اقسم بربّ المشارق و المغارب"

2- The Easts and the Wests are under Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah). "ربّ المشارق و المغارب"

3- The cosmic system is founded upon order and wisdom, such that the risings and settings of every day in every place can be calculated precisely. "المشارق و المغارب"

4- Both creation and its replacement are in His hand. "خلقناهم... نبدّل خیراً منهم"

5- Never consider yourselves the best, lest Allah bring forth someone better than you. "نُبدّل خیراً"

6- Guidance is necessary as long as there is a possibility of effect, but after despairing of their guidance, they must be left alone. "فذرهم"

7- Vain and baseless talk about religion and religious leaders plunges man into the swamp of sin and makes him preoccupied and trapped. "یخوضوا و یلعبوا"

8- The duty of invitation and guidance continues as long as there is a potential for acceptance, but if they engage in vain talk and treat everything as a game, they should be left to their own devices. "ذرهم یخوضوا و یلعبوا"

9- Leaving stubborn people alone is equivalent to granting them respite. "فذرهم یخوضوا"

10- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical. "یخرجون من الاجداث"

11- Speed in the massive crowd is a sign of the terror and anxiety of the disbelievers on the Day of Judgment. "سراعاً"

12- Disgrace and humiliation on the Day of Judgment will encompass the entire being of the disbelievers. "ترهقهم ذلّة"

13- The Prophets always warned people about the Day of Judgment. "یومهم الّذى یوعدون... الیوم الّذى كانوا یوعدون"
